system: Ghana Health Service Referral Tier System
description: Ghana's public health system operates on a tiered referral model. Patients should enter at the lowest appropriate level and be referred upward as needed.

tiers.name: CHPS Compound
tiers.description: The first point of contact for most Ghanaians, especially in rural and peri-urban areas. Staffed by Community Health Officers (CHOs) and nurses.
typical_services: Treatment of minor illnesses (malaria, respiratory infections, wounds)
Referrals to higher level when needed

tiers.name: Health Centre / Polyclinic
tiers.description: Broader primary care with some diagnostic capacity. Managed by medical officers and nurses. Found at sub-district level.

tiers.name: District / Municipal Hospital
tiers.description: Full inpatient hospital with emergency services. Managed by medical officers and specialists. Found at district level.

tiers.name: Regional / Teaching Hospital
tiers.description: Tertiary and specialist care. Includes Korle-Bu Teaching Hospital, Komfo Anokye Teaching Hospital, Tamale Teaching Hospital, and regional hospitals.

emergency_contacts.note: Numbers are illustrative for this educational project. Verify real GHS contacts before clinical use.

referral_principles: Always refer with a written referral note describing the patient's condition and what treatment has been given.
Critical patients should be stabilised before referral where possible.
A community member or family member should always accompany a referred patient.
The receiving facility should be called ahead when possible.
NHIS (National Health Insurance Scheme) covers services at all levels — patients should carry their card.
